note: Pre-populated narrative fixtures for the canned Pages demo. Keys match seed-pnl.ts line IDs; exec key is reserved for the close summary.

rev-dtc.commentary: Direct-to-Consumer revenue rose $135M (+5.0%) to $2.85B, led by 3% volume growth and a 2% price uplift across premium lines. FX was a modest 0.5% headwind but mix improvement of 0.5% largely neutralized it. Performance aligns with the channel-investment thesis; no one-time items flagged.

rev-wholesale.commentary: Wholesale revenue declined $175M (-4.1%) to $4.12B, driven by -2% volume contraction tied to key-account destocking in North America. EMEA remained resilient. No accounting or disclosure concerns, but concentration exposure to top 3 accounts warrants continued monitoring.

rev-licensing.commentary: Licensing & royalty revenue grew $14M (+10.9%) to $142M, propelled by new licensee expansion in Greater China and favorable royalty-rate mix. Growth is consistent with prior quarters' trajectory; no disclosure triggers identified.

cogs-product.commentary: Product cost of sales increased $125M (+3.4%) to $3.84B despite 1.5% material deflation, as higher freight rates and a shift toward premium SKUs offset input savings. Margin compression is modest but trending; procurement should validate freight-cost trajectory for Q3.
risk_flags: Material deflation may reverse; monitor commodity exposure

cogs-logistics.commentary: Logistics and distribution costs decreased $35M (-8.3%) to $385M, benefiting from lower ocean freight rates and improved DC throughput in EMEA. Favorable variance reflects operational wins; trend expected to stabilize in H2.

opex-demand-creation.commentary: Demand creation expense grew $130M (+12.4%) to $1.18B, reflecting front-loaded campaign investment for the Q2 product launch and an 18% YoY increase in digital spend. Spend is above plan; ROAS measurement will be critical at the next variance review.
risk_flags: Above plan — validate ROAS against campaign KPIs

opex-sga.commentary: Selling, general, and administrative expense grew $53M (+3.1%) to $1.75B, consistent with FX-neutral wage inflation (~3%) and a planned 4% corporate headcount investment. No unusual items identified.

opex-rd.commentary: Research, design, and development expense rose $16M (+9.9%) to $178M, driven by the innovation center expansion and a new materials science initiative. Growth rate is above aggregate opex (~3%); strategic rather than operational.

opex-store-ops.commentary: Store operations expense grew $17M (+4.3%) to $412M, with lease expense up 4% from renewals and labor up 3% at minimum-wage locations. Increases align with prior guidance; no disclosure triggers.

opex-da.commentary: Depreciation and amortization rose $16M (+8.1%) to $214M, reflecting technology modernization capex coming online and DC automation rollouts. Trajectory matches the capex plan communicated in the FY budget.

below-interest.commentary: Net interest expense rose $13M (+37.1%) to $48M on higher short-term borrowings and an elevated rate environment. Movement is consistent with treasury's funding strategy; no refinancing events this quarter.
risk_flags: Review whether to lock longer-duration debt

below-other.commentary: Other income/(expense) swung $40M unfavorably to ($22M), driven by FX translation losses on APLA exposure and the prior-period gain on an asset sale not repeating. Majority of variance is non-operating.
key_drivers: No repeat of prior asset-sale gain
risk_flags: FX exposure may persist — treasury to evaluate hedging

below-tax.commentary: Income tax expense declined $14M (-5.8%) to $228M with an effective rate of 20.8% vs 21.6% prior, aided by discrete benefits in EMEA. Rate trend favorable; discrete items should be disclosed per SEC guidance.

exec.headline: Q2 FY26 close completed in 5.2 days — 35% ahead of target — with DTC strength offsetting wholesale destocking.
key_highlights: Close cycle: 5.2 days vs 8-day target; auto-certification rate 82%
Demand creation spend +$130M YoY — campaign investment front-loaded
Gross margin compression modest (~40bps) from freight + premium mix
risks: FX translation pressure on APLA exposure — $22M unfavorable below-line
Demand creation spend above plan; validate ROAS before Q3
AWS Enterprise embedded-derivative (CPI escalator) ASC 815 review required
exec.recommendation: Proceed to consolidation; flag AWS ASC 815 review and APLA FX hedging strategy for controller steering committee.
